symptom_text: plants show root rot and wilting symptoms
disease_context: Soil-borne oomycete diseases thrive in poorly drained soils and spread through waterlogged conditions.
severity_hint: severe in waterlogged soils, can wipe out entire fields

symptom_text: small brown sclerotia and white fungal mycelium appear at the stem base
disease_context: Fungal diseases caused by sclerotia persist in soil and infect plants at the collar region under warm humid conditions.
severity_hint: kills plants rapidly at the collar region

symptom_text: plant shows wilting, stem browning and sticky bacterial ooze from stem
disease_context: Bacterial diseases spread through water, insects, and contaminated tools and develop quickly in warm humid conditions.
severity_hint: can cause rapid plant death if not treated early

symptom_text: black sunken lesions appear on fruits and stems
disease_context: Anthracnose fungi spread through infected plant debris and rain splash, causing fruit rot and stem lesions.
severity_hint: major post-harvest loss, reduces market value

symptom_text: younger leaves show dwarfing and stunted growth
disease_context: Viral diseases are transmitted by insect vectors like aphids and thrips, leading to stunted growth and leaf distortion.
severity_hint: reduces yield and quality, persists across seasons

symptom_text: roots develop galls and knots due to nematode infestation
disease_context: Nematode infestations cause root galls, reducing nutrient uptake and plant vigor, especially in sandy soils.
severity_hint: chronic yield loss, plants remain stunted
